reviews.review_description: My friend and I were addicted to it, it's a super fun gun game for the Oculus Go. I think it is the most realistic zombie game on this VR. I thought the game would be really scary, but it wasn't that bad. The game reminds me of Call Of Duty Zombies but VR version!

developer_response.body: Hi DerekImme, Thank you for your comment. We’re very glad that you liked the game.

reviews.review_description: No Pre buy warning you have to be in a swivel chair (which I don't have)  or stand up for the entire game,  pretty lame.   I died fast bc I couldn't turn around to shoot the zombies hitting me from behind.  I guess developers are ignorant to the fact that some people don't have a swivel chair or some of us work all day and want to sit down and relax.   I'll revisit this game if I ever have the energy to stand and play it or the devs get smart and introduce a quick turn button.

developer_response.body:
Hey U-Go-6Ft-Below, 

Thank you for your comment. 

We are really sorry that you faced difficulties. Yes, actually you’re right in your comment that the game doesn’t have an option for fast turning of a player. That's exactly why when designing the gameplay we were doing our best to make enemies appear within a player’s visibility scope (within a radius of about 140 degrees in front of him), and we intentionally minimized situations in which enemies are generated behind the back of a player. 

Maybe, due to different game scenarios (e.g. the order and speed of interaction with enemies), we couldn’t achieve this in all game moments, but we definitely tried in every possible way to minimize such situations.

At the end of the second scene (when you’re crossing bridges) there is the moment in the game with the corridor turn behind a player’s back, but you don’t necessarily have to change the body position completely – you can just activate the next point and return the body position to convenient state, and when the game goes to the third scene (after Loading screen) the game geometry will be recentered (the game path will be in front of you). 

After this situation, the game doesn't have necessary turns behind a player’s back. 

Also, try to select an easier game mode (Easy), in this case, you’ll need fewer movements and decision-making time in order to complete the game. We hope that this comment will be able to help a bit.

Also, at our end, we’ll think how is it possible to implement the functionality for turning of a player using a controller. Maybe, in the next updates, we’ll add an additional mode.

Thank you once again for your comment, it is really useful for us.

Should you have any additional questions or ideas – please, write us about this at info@dream-dev.com. Thanks.

reviews.review_description: i'm unable to create an avatar which is required to start game...new go user :(.    *Update I used sideload launcher and it works now, sweet game!

developer_response.body:
Hi Marukhet,
Thank you for your comment.
Actually, avatar is not required to play the game. We recommend you to contact Oculus Support Service: https://support.oculus.com/articles/contact-us/contact-us/index-contact-us
Hopefully, they will help to solve the problem.

reviews.review_title: Well done but ... a bit dull
reviews.review_description: This is a well made game. Plays well, but it just gets repetitive and samey real fast. It needs better pacing and maybe more random encounters. It needs to slow things down and make things creepy rather than just keep throwing on wave after wave of zombies in a predefined pattern.
